title: Research
text: My research examines how firms and individuals respond to environmental regulations, credit market conditions, and fiscal policy — and the implications of these responses for environmental quality, productivity, and economic welfare. A secondary strand of work addresses health, human capital, and political economy.

title: Teaching
text:
Teaching
I teach undergraduate and graduate courses in environmental economics, natural resource economics, and quantitative methods at the University of Alberta.
Course materials — slides, problem sets, and announcements — are available to enrolled students via the student portal.


  
    Econ 365
    Natural Resource Economics
    Economics of non-renewable and renewable resources, extraction theory, resource policy, and sustainability.
  

  
    Econ 467
    Environmental Economics
    Market failures, externalities, environmental regulation, cost-benefit analysis, and climate policy.
  

  
    Econ 566
    Environmental Economics
    Graduate-level environmental economics.

title: Dana C. Andersen
text:
Associate Professor · Department of Economics · University of Alberta
My research interests are in environmental and resource economics, public economics, and sustainable development. Much of my work examines how firms respond to environmental regulations, credit market conditions, and tax policy — and the consequences of these responses for environmental quality, productivity, and labor markets.
    
      dca@ualberta.ca
       ·  9-23 HM Tory Building, University of Alberta, Edmonton AB T6G 2H4
